_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-17 Patient Procedural Sedation (Revised 2/24/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-17: Patient Procedural Sedation — MDHHS Approved 2/24/2023
purpose: PARAMEDIC USE ONLY. Procedural sedation is authorized for: electrical therapy (cardioversion and TCP), post-intubation sedation, and CPAP/HFNC with DIRECT Medical Control order. CPAP/HFNC sedation: KETAMINE IS NOT PERMITTED for this indication. Only ONE MCA-authorized medication may be given pre-radio contact; Medical Control must be contacted if a different medication is needed.

sections.title: Authorized Indications
points: Electrical therapy: synchronized cardioversion or transcutaneous pacing (TCP)
CPAP or HFNC: ONLY with direct Medical Control order — KETAMINE IS NOT PERMITTED for this indication

sections.title: Pre-Radio Contact Authorization
points: Only ONE MCA-authorized sedation medication may be given prior to Medical Control contact
If the clinical situation requires a medication OTHER than the pre-radio authorized medication: contact Medical Control first
Document all sedation medications, doses, times, and patient response

sections.title: Adult Sedation Options
points: MIDAZOLAM (Versed): 1–5 mg (0.05 mg/kg) IV/IO; titrate slowly; IN if IV not available; repeat once in 5 minutes to maximum 0.1 mg/kg
DIAZEPAM (Valium): 5–10 mg (0.1 mg/kg) IV/IO; repeat every 5 minutes to maximum 0.3 mg/kg
FENTANYL: 50–100 mcg (1 mcg/kg) IV/IO; repeat every 4 minutes to maximum 3 mcg/kg
KETAMINE: 4 mg/kg IM OR 1.5 mg/kg IV/IO; IN if IV not available; maximum 500 mg; NOT for CPAP or HFNO

sections.title: Pediatric Sedation Options
points: Pediatric dosing per MI-MEDIC cards when available
BACKUP MIDAZOLAM: 0.05 mg/kg IV/IO (IN if IV not available); repeat once to maximum 0.1 mg/kg
BACKUP FENTANYL: 1 mcg/kg IV/IO (IN if IV not available); repeat every 5 minutes to maximum 3 mcg/kg
BACKUP KETAMINE: 4 mg/kg IM OR 1.5 mg/kg IV/IO; NOT for CPAP or HFNO

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select which sedation medication(s) are authorized for Paramedic use prior to Medical Control contact for procedural sedation in adults
options: Midazolam 1–5 mg IV/IO (0.05 mg/kg, titrate, repeat once to max 0.1 mg/kg)
Diazepam 5–10 mg IV/IO (0.1 mg/kg, repeat q5 min to max 0.3 mg/kg)
Fentanyl 50–100 mcg IV/IO (1 mcg/kg, repeat q4 min to max 3 mcg/kg)
Ketamine 4 mg/kg IM or 1.5 mg/kg IV/IO (max 500 mg — NOT for CPAP/HFNO)

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select which sedation medication(s) are authorized for Paramedic use prior to Medical Control contact for procedural sedation in pediatric patients
options: Midazolam per MI-MEDIC / backup 0.05 mg/kg IV/IO, repeat once to max 0.1 mg/kg
Fentanyl per MI-MEDIC / backup 1 mcg/kg IV/IO, repeat q5 min to max 3 mcg/kg
Ketamine per MI-MEDIC / backup 4 mg/kg IM or 1.5 mg/kg IV/IO — NOT for CPAP/HFNO

key_drugs.name: Midazolam (Procedural Sedation — Adult)
key_drugs.dose: 1–5 mg (0.05 mg/kg) IV/IO titrated slowly; IN if IV unavailable; repeat once in 5 min to max 0.1 mg/kg
key_drugs.indication: Procedural sedation for electrical therapy, post-intubation sedation, or CPAP/HFNC (MC order required for CPAP/HFNC)
key_drugs.contraindications: Titrate slowly; monitor airway and SpO2; only one pre-radio medication per MCA selection
key_drugs.notes: Adult maximum via any route: 0.1 mg/kg. Titrate slowly to effect. Only authorized for CPAP/HFNC with direct MC order.

key_drugs.name: Ketamine (Procedural Sedation)
key_drugs.dose: 4 mg/kg IM OR 1.5 mg/kg IV/IO; IN if IV unavailable; maximum 500 mg
key_drugs.indication: Procedural sedation for electrical therapy or post-intubation sedation
key_drugs.contraindications: NOT for CPAP or HFNO; NOT for patient restraint without online MC
key_drugs.side_effects: Emergence phenomena, nausea, laryngospasm (with rapid IV), increased secretions
key_drugs.notes: KETAMINE IS NOT PERMITTED for CPAP or HFNO sedation.

deterioration_flags: Respiratory depression after sedation — assist ventilation; maintain airway; have reversal agents available (flumazenil for benzodiazepines, naloxone for opioids)
Different sedation medication needed than pre-radio authorized — contact Medical Control before administering
CPAP/HFNC sedation needed — contact Medical Control; ketamine NOT permitted for this indication
scope_notes: Indications: electrical therapy (cardioversion/TCP), post-intubation sedation, CPAP/HFNC (MC order required for CPAP/HFNC)
Pre-radio: only one MCA-authorized medication permitted; contact MC for different medication
Adult: midazolam, diazepam, fentanyl, or ketamine per MCA selection
Pediatric: per MI-MEDIC; backup midazolam, fentanyl, or ketamine per MCA selection
